zh.lifeSummary: Mary Barbara Drech與Gerhard Will Willis結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mary Barbara Drech与Gerhard Will Willis结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Drech
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Brach   1 or   Brecht  .

variants.name: Brach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Cheskel, Moshe, Chaim, Mendel, Pinchas, Bernat, Hershel, Meyer, Moche, Mort, Rivka, Rivky.

German: topographic name from Middle High German brache ‘fallow land, pastureland’, originally ‘newly plowed land’. Alternatively, a habitational name from any of the places called Braach (Hesse) or Brach (Baden-Württemberg, Franconia).

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Barbara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

variants.name: Barbra
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.
